title: vaccinium myrtillus subsp. myrtillus
intro: Vaccinium myrtillus subsp. myrtillus, also known as the bilberry, is a perennial shrub species belonging to the heath family. It is native to Europe, northern Asia, and northern Africa, and is prized for its dark blue berries, which are rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants with medicinal properties. The bilberry has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, scurvy, and inflammation, due to its high content of vitamins C and P, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Bilberry is often prepared as a tea, jam, or dried powder, and can also be consumed as a supplement or added to food products, offering a range of health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, enhanced vision, and boosted immune function.
